meaning: n.到处存在,(同时的)普遍存在
examples: Last, but definitely not least, is Ubiquity.
Their marketing power is diminished by ubiquity.
The ubiquity of the quality culture should extend to the customer.
This ubiquity of female Asian models begs the question, Where are their male counterparts?
Businesses can either suffer from that ubiquity or try to take advantage of it.
